evals.name: library-eval-detection
evals.prompt: We're picking a schema validation library for our TypeScript backend. Should we use Zod or Yup? Research it properly.
evals.expected_output: Skill activates, auto-detects the 'library-eval' mode, states the mode + a one-line interpretation, identifies reader role, and runs the 5 library-eval lenses (Maintainer-Health, Production-Operator, Security, Performance, Cost-License). Primary output is an HTML briefing + scored MD. Citations carry version/date and trace to primary sources (OSV, GitHub, official docs).

evals.name: architecture-adr-detection
evals.prompt: I need to write an ADR for whether our orders service should use event sourcing or a plain CRUD model. We expect ~5k orders/day, a 4-person team, and we already run Postgres.
evals.expected_output: Skill activates, auto-detects the 'architecture' mode, runs the 5 architecture lenses (Scalability-Perf, Reliability-Operability, Security, Cost-TCO, Team-Fit-Risk) framed on ISO 25010 + ATAM trade-off points. Primary output is a MADR-style ADR markdown (Context, Decision Drivers, Considered Options, Decision Outcome, Consequences, Pros/Cons per option, More Information) with rejected options justified; HTML secondary.

evals.name: ambiguous-mode-asks
evals.prompt: Research upgrading and re-architecting our GraphQL layer.
evals.expected_output: The query matches both 'migration' (upgrading) and 'architecture' (re-architecting) with similar confidence, so per Phase 0 the skill should ASK the user which mode rather than silently guessing, offering the matching modes with one-line descriptions.
